text:
I would like to take this opportunity to write a review of Sprint/T-Mobile.
I am writing this negative review regarding the overall customer service that I've received over the last 2 1/2 months.
To begin I've been paying (Automatic payments) since 2016. I recently had to get a new ATM card. I received my new card. I contacted Sprint online chat to setup my new card, and to also change the plan to a less expensive plan for my Mother, and myself. I also had been trying to get Sprint/T-Mobile to honor their warranty on my defective Samsung Note 20 Ultra. They finally agreed to honor their warranty. I got my replacement. The phone has been working flawlessly. When I contacted Sprint/T-Mobile thru online chat, I was in contact with a C.S.R. named Hazel. We discussed changing feom the family share plan to the Essentials 55 plan. I authorized the change to begin 8/1/2022. I also had her setup my new ATM card for automatic payments to continue on the original 1st of each month to be deducted from my Credit Union Account. I come to find out today (8/12/2022) that the new plan, and automatic payments were NOT setup as promised by Hazel. I chatted with a supposed supervisor named Justin this morning. He refused to explain to me why my new plan had NOT been started 8/1/2022., and why my automatic payments were NOT setup as promised by Hazel on 7/22/2022. I AM NOT HAPPY WITH SPRINT/T-MOBILE FOR NOT TAKING CARE OF THEIR CUSTOMERS. AS THEY ALL PROMISED!

I TRULY HOPE THIS EXPERIENCE WILL ASSIST OTHERS WHO ARE DEBATING ON WEATHER TO JOIN A SERVICE THAT TEULY DOES NOT KNOW HOW TO TAKE CARE OF THEIR CUSTOMERS

GOOD LUCK TO ALL WHO READ THIS REVIEW.

Thank you so much Miranda for helping me retrieved all my emails and contact numbers on my cell phones. You are the best! T mobile is lucky to have you you as part of their team! I really appreciate your help! Thank you!
Worst company ever! I returned my equipment less than 24 hours after receiving it.. They sent me a bill for $85.00. I am filling a complaint with the Attorney General.

Constant missing text messages that are discovered only after talking with friends and family. The 3G hotspot throttles to 600kbps (extremely slow) after a few emails with attachments and software updates. Streaming TV via hotspot is not possible. LTE coverage (in Washington DC/Baltimore area of all places) is slow and spotty despite being near their towers meaning they're likely over tasking towers. $35 fee for in-store support.

They are liars, they will tell you what you want to hear.....then crickets (nothing) when you complain.  I cancelled my service in early March and they are still STEALING from me 4 months later.
I was a customer over a decade and am appalled at their theft from a disabled, senior veteran.  I have used nothing of theirs since March 4th and after changing debit cards, in July they turned me over to collections.....what could I owe them for?  Can't ask them as they just lie!

If you decide getting T-mobile service, make sure you dont believe whatever the sales person says. Do not trust them. They are there to sell and the more they sell, the more commision they make. They are not honest, they try to rob you by just keep saying it is free. Make sure to find out about the monthly charges, equipment return period, and what is the consequence of something which was offered to you for free in case you dont like it. I got robbed, and I am stucked with huge monthly bill for two years. I wish I would never walked to the T Mobile store. What a mistake I made. 
Customer service is no help at all. Don't get fooled by their custormer service. They dont solve your issue. They just try justify the bill. If you are not happy, customer service will not give you an inch. I am angry as hell. No one helped me and I can not dispute the charges or cancel the service. I wish I could give a zero star. pathetic customer service, pathetic sales person, and phatetic manager who I spoke to and watested an hour of my time. I am more frustrated now.

T-Mobile - just don't!!!  We've had nothing but issues with them trying to get and after we received a mobile hotspot for full-time traveling in our RV.  First off, the t-mobile store in Rapid City, SD required us to jump through all kinds of hoops because we are SD residents and share a street address with 20k others who travel full time - they wouldn't or couldn't understand that we have a unique box number at that address.  After we jumped through all the hoops and made multiple trips to the store, they totally dropped the ball and we did not get the hotspot and the sales person would not return my calls (we had left the area).   
We tried again a few weeks later in our next location, going the online route.  We successfully got the device - it never worked for more than just a few minutes to a few hours at any one time.  We called their joke of customer care on numerous occasions and they kept bouncing us around to their "specialized tech" and telling us it was this or that issue and now it would work - never did.  Once, the tech had us reset to factory settings and 3, yes THREE minutes later had the same issue.  They even sent us to a store an hour away to get a new SIM card as they insisted we had a bad card - didn't work for more than 25 minutes.       
Next, after we insisted that the device itself must be faulty, they promised to send us a new unit and promised we'd receive it on a particular day and they would call us back at a specific time two days later to verify shipping.  Neither happened.  Finally, the day after we were to have received the new device, we called to cancel the account and tell them we were not going to pay, either for the device that had not worked or for the service thus far.  Of course, they said we still have to pay, but we are going to fight that.... stay tuned.... #tmobilesucks!!!

Horrible service, could not use my phone unless I left my house and drove out of my subdivision .. I called to complain about their service and they wanted to reduce my bill , and assured me the towers would be ready in three days!!🙄🙄..After three days they needed more time to work on the towers .. I took the new phones we purchased and returned them.. It was within their fourteen day grace period  , but was told I would not get my phone back I traded in .. and they would take 110.00 out of my down payment for a restocking fee.. Totally rip off Company with the worst service ever !!! Run from this place ..

READ THIS BEFORE YOU COSIDER T-MOBILE. 
 I needed a new phone immediately because mine was no longer working. It was past the warranty and I was going to go to "Cricket,"  They were going to give me a much better deal on service and a free phone.  I was paying almost $70 could never get T-Mobile to work with me. Cricket offers $30 for the same plan.  I called T-Mobile to get information and let them know I would pay what little I owe on my phone so that I could leave. They decided then they could actually lower my bill. They also were going to give me a  Free phone to match cricket. Even though my warranty was no longer valid, to keep me a customer because I had been there since 2012. I was hesitant but I agreed because they gave me a good deal and was going to ship a phone to my home within 2 days. That was July 29th it is now August 10th and I have no phone and have been told Multiple times a phone was mailed to me. Later to be told that they canceled it because I was no longer under warranty.  Even though I explained and gave them the confirmation number that they were going to give me a free phone to keep me a customer so that I did not go to cricket they would always reset up a plan and then someone else would cancel it. They decided to give me $300, To go buy a phone somewhere else. Then I could stay a customer there. They sent the money to someone else's card.  I was told I would have to wait until they recouped the money before they would help me. I've tried to close out my account and they will hang up the phone on me constantly. They tell me that they're going to help me every time I call and they always hang up. I missed a call of one passing away, Multiple social engagements, etc.  I am out a lot of money. I have even done running that T-Mobile has asked. They have made an absolute fool of me. This could be you. When they tell you they're going to take care of you and you need your phone will you miss a phone call for your child because they will not provide you service like they did me? I still have no phone yet they still bill me. When they talked to me about the death  And losses they act like it's an everyday thing. This could be you if you joined them, What will happen if your child needs you and you don't have your phone or they don't have their service?  If anyone is having problems like me you need to call the federal trade commission in your State, the utilities commission,  Your state attorney.  File complaints with them because T-Mobile will make promises to you like they did me and then they will not follow through. They will tell you they want to earn your trust they will tell you that they are the last person you will have to talk to. I am still without my cell phone and they Hang up on me when I ask them to close out my account so I can go to another cell company. I have never been so mistreated in my entire life by buy a cell service company. Run from t-mobile.

Wow. After agreeing to a $350m settlement, you would think that T-Mobile would understand security. Not so. I went to my local T-Mobile store to switch to them. They had me enter all my personal information in their online system, including taking images of my drivers license and having me enter my SSN. That wasn't enough - they got me on the phone and said that I needed to confirm my identity. So, in a store literally full of people, they asked me to tell them (on speakerphone!) my full name. Then they asked for my address. Then they asked me to repeat - out loud, on an unsecured line - my full SSN. The woman who had been helping me said, "isn't it enough that I have it in the system?", but it wasn't. The upshot was, "we're terminating this procedure." AKA, go somewhere else. Does anyone else have a problem announcing your full name, address, and social in front of something like 15 people? If this is how they deal with data security BEFORE they get you to sign up, I can only imagine how they deal with it afterwards.

Was a customer over 7 years and never even had a late payment. In December 2021 they shut off my service without notifying me because according to them I made a $800 international call while in the Florida keys.. by the way it was an in-bound call from Florida. I called them to see what was going on since all of a sudden I couldn't call, text, or use the internet. They said it was an error and would be fixing everything. Fast forward a month when I get a bill for the $800 call.. so I call them again and they said they could only credit me $300. I told them if that was the case I would be canceling my service since it was an error on their end. I paid the high bill and then in February 2022 canceled my service and paid off the 2 phones on my 2 lines. Fast forward to August 2022 when I get a bill for $60 for a plan I don't even have?!? Turns out they didn't cancel one of the lines then turned around and said I owed more on one of the phones. I said send me all my bills since I got the new phones so I can review it myself. They claimed they couldn't send me my bills.. keep in mind I can't log into Tmobile.com... because the account can't be found. I spent over 2 hours on the phone because the first rep put me back in the call loop after keeping me on the phone for over an hour. Keep in mind I was very nice and patient. The second time I called he tried to say I owed money for the phone. I asked for a supervisor and finally spoke to someone who could actually help me. She reviewed everything and gave me the numbers, sure enough, the phone was bought Jan 2021 and had a 24 month term for Samsung to give me the phone for free. $799.99 minus 13 payments of $33.34 ($433.42) left a balance of $366.57... which is the amount I paid in February when I canceled both lines. So in the end they charged me a bogus fee for an incoming call then when I canceled my service they continued to bill me for zero reason and then claimed I still owed them money when I didn't. Such a waste of 2 hours and wow is the customer service horrible. Do not do business with them, they are SHADY!

Was told we would be able to pay 100 within 48 hours to restore our service 101% promised and had the money within 24 hours and had 1 person take an hour to ask me the same question and another person said it’s now 189.00. She said that’s what it’s at now. So I asked even though the person yesterday was 101% sure we could pay $100 today. They said yes it’s 189.00 today. Sent screenshots to that last person and literally shows the 101% promise. So trust TMobile on their word if you here for a short time. I’m looking into other carriers if anyone has and recommendations.

I normally have great things to say about T-Mobile because I have used them for years and never really had issues. I decided to change my line to business since I mainly use it business. This is the beginning of the end! Lets add to the insult, I purchased a brand new $800 phone at the time too! Nothing has been right since! It was not a smooth transaction at all. I had to pay for a sim card for line that I already had. They told me they had to make a new line to create an account for business, but don't fear I will be able to keep my same number? SO I paid $15 (sim card) to keep my same number! They created another number just to remove it. When they removed it, of course they took away my number and I was out of service. I had no form of communication whatsoever. I had to call from my work cell to let them know. NO ONE KNEW WHAT THEY WERE DOING. They then sent someone TO MY HOUSE with a new sim card to get my phone to work. Once it started working again, the internet access was TERRIBLE. I called to ask what the issue was with the internet and was told my area seems to have trouble and they were working on it. This is all within the 1st 2 weeks! I have never called this company so much in my life. I have intermittent service, poor customer service, promises of callbacks that never happen and still poor service. I recently spoke to an agent that empathized with the situation and offered $50 off my bill after I saw they charged me $385 for something that she could not reconcile. Lets not even mention I can not access the online account services. That seems to be botched as well. I have never been so unhappy with mobile service like this. All because I wanted it under my business! This company is the WORST and keep in mind this is only the 1st month of business service change.

this should be simple, husband passed away, took death record the tmobile store and have called 3 times, no turning off the phone, i can tell you  a dead man is not going to be using his phone, what a waste of customer service.  Phil Stevens 206-209-8984

ABSOLUTE WORST SERVICE! They say that 5G is amazing, and more towers, and all that good stuff, but no. I wish Sprint had never merged because I had way better service with Sprint than I do now, with a 5G phone. I literally have no service ever unless I'm connected to wifi, which obviously isn't their service. Point blank, t-mobile sucks. That's being honest.

I just called to get a new screen cover shipped to me. First she was going to charge me $40 plus shipping. When I questioned why because I have insurance she then placed me on hold checking in about every 10min to tell me she was working on it. I was on hold for over an hour for the call to mysteriously drop. She did not even call me back. I cannot stand TMOBILE!!!! Both customer and cell service are horrible!!!

I had T-Mobile for 8 years and when I left the service I made sure to pay everything off. After 3 months of canceling the service they sent me a notice that I had a credit balance. I called and let them know my previous card was no longer active and yet they refused to return my money any other way. So now I overpaid and cannot get my $100 back, basically I’ve been robbed.

My experience with T-Mobile has been mixed but mostly bad. The first time we went to switch from another company, they gave us a good deal but made us wait four hours at the store because there were tech issues. They gave us free charging blocks to compensate but one of them didn’t work. When we got our first bill I was shocked that it was so much more than they quoted. It turned out we got Asurion insurance without realizing it. That was fine until my son’s phone got stolen and they wouldn’t replace it because they said his phone wasn’t insured, only mine and my husband’s which makes total sense, right?!? In any case, we were so distraught about its and pretty much gave up until someone from customer service called us and said they were going to send us a replacement for free as a courtesy since we just switched. We were elated and I asked if I could tell my son. He said yes. My mistake was I didn’t get his name and just waited for his email like he said. It never came. We never got the phone. Why make promises you can’t keep T-Mobile?!?! If I could switch now I would but is too much time I don’t have. I’ll give them a year and see. Not happy about these dropped calls and unsent text messages!!!

Just doesn’t work abroad. No signal whatsoever. 

Clearly I did everything I would usually do in this situation, read manuals and talked to customer service. 


Every other SIM card works without any problems. T-Mobile just wouldn’t connect.


Never ever again. I swear some cheap alternatives just work better

I recently purchased an iPhone 13 at the T-Mobile store in Lakewood, Colorado located at 7850 W Alameda Avenue Suite A.  This pleasant experience was due to the help and guidance of Mary Deibert.  She took care of all the details to help me receive the 55+ plan and give me a service plan that is affordable and efficient for me.  Thank you Mary for listening to the needs of your customers and taking care of each of us with our specific and unique situations!  Go visit this store if you live in this area.  You will be happy you did!

I brought my (7) Sprint cell phones in to get converted to T-Mobile. I upgraded (4) phones and was to get a $400 trade in credit. Only (3) phones received the credit. I have called the store several times and was promised that Jamal the store manage would call back. NEVER happened. I call the Customer Complaint line several times and got several incident numbers. I was told the BACK OFFICE would have to investigate. No return call. 9 months later I am still waiting for the call.
T-Mobile must instruct their Customer Service reps to tell the customer that they will have to call you back.
Stay away from T-Mobile.

Since T mobile switched to foreign non-English speaking reps it's customer service has plummeted. A simple disconnection of one number and transfer to a different number took two weeks. They just couldn't get it right. I had a terrible time even just communicating with the so called "expert". Terrible experience.

I purchased 2 iphone 12 promax one for me and one for my wife from T-mobile. The one for me stop working, it wouldn't turn on. I called T-Mobile for help and they referred me to one of their stores for repairs. I took the phone to the store they referred me to and after looking at the phone they said they couldn't repair it. They then referred me to an Apple store for the repair. So I took the phone to and Apple store to have Apple take a look at it, and was told the repair would cost me $500.00. So instead of paying $500.00 I purchased a new iphone 13 Promax from Apple. The next day I get an email from the T-Mobile store saying my phone had been repaired and I could come in a pick it up and the estimated repair fee was $279.00 payable by credit card. Keep in mind this the store that said they couldn't repair the phone and the phone was never left at the store. IS THIS ONE SCREWED UP COMPANY OR WHAT! I DO NOT RECOMMEND ANYONE  BUYING ANYTHING FROM T-Mobile. THIS IS ONE SORRY COMPANY!!!!!!!!!

The 611 service is horrible. My free Samsung Galaxy phone crashed on the screen and I called 611 T mobile service. The representative name Grace who was a woman serviced me. She said I need buy the insurance then they will give me a new phone, she said call me Monday at 1pm, but I didn't receive any phone call from here, then I called again, this time is Irina answered me, she checked out for me and said Grace didn't put me in the insurance. I was very mad at Grace. I waited for one week, why you didn't do the business for me, she is liar, horrible. I am still waiting for the phone, not sure when can I received,  but I will talk to thr perisent if they didn't help me.

Horrible service. Will always lose service at my work place even when I’m always there and it works one moment and the next it completely stops no internet or anything and when you try to talk to a rep to fix it they would tell you to try to look at your account or do something on the internet but how the hell can I do that when my internet in my phone isn’t working like no common sense. Not to mention if you cancel your service early they won’t refund you! Even if it’s the beginning of the month like that’s bull**** and they’ll charge you next month for no damn reason and they lock you out of the account like are you guys stupid. I would have never known about it if I didn’t review my bank statement. Worse carrier ever. And they tell me they can’t refund me the for the current bill they charge me for that I shouldn’t have been charge and they even say I’m not sure why you got charged. Like the h***

Ordered SIM card, selected plan, then ported #, receive text showing charge for $25.00 higher plan, from date I ordered card, not when # ported. Call that day and told will be corrected within 24-48 hours.  Received text 3 weeks later showing $65 charge.  Call that day and say the credit will be on the next bill.  This is a pattern that these globalist companies use to fraudulently charge you, keep your money for additional time and then credit, if you remember to verify you received it.

Their claim to have the best 5G service in the nation is a complete joke. Price swayed me to give them a try after salesman swore we had coverage where we live. Had little to no service not only on my phone but also on their G5 Gateway internet.

T-Mobile got me in there with their 55 and older deal. However I wasn’t 55 at the time, I was only 7 months away. They told me the 3rd line was free for life. And yes, I have it in writing. This was not the case. Once I turned 55 we upgraded our paid off phones we brought with us. Once the first bill come in, they started charging us for the line. I have everything from the day we started with t mobile to the current moment in writing on text message. This company is none other then trash. I highly recommend staying away from them!

Pitiful customer service.  Nothing promised during the 'sales pitch' was delivered.  I went back to the store to find out why and I was told I waited too long past the 14 day period where they could help me.  The worst rip-off I've experienced in many years.  outright lying to the potential customer, much like buying a used car from a guy on the streetcorner
Terrible. Had sprint for years wasn't good but not as bad as now. Coverage sucks my data is throttled for hours right in the middle of the day.

T mobile is getting to be the worst phone company I have dealt with no customer service. Representative lie and say they will call you back,they don’t. They made me set up payment arrangements but when I set it up I had an extra $200 dollars add to my bill. I call to inquire. The representative told me she could not remove it because it was already put in the payment arrangement. I said well I’m not paying nothing extra in my payment arrangement. T-Mobile is a ripoff.I have been back and forth on the phone with T-Mobile for 2 months. If you have more than one line on the phone please watch you bill. I don’t care if they let you set up payment arrangement and you can pay at a later date. That’s a set up. Watch your bill.  I will be moving my 4 lines somewhere else. 
  P.S. don’t let the representative tell you they will call you back. They always lie. Your phone will get disconnected waiting on them.
I have been on hold for a total of 4 hours!! This is absolutely insane!! Also the coverage is horrible!!

We have been with t mobil for approx 6 mo.
I call and asked for a payment extension till the 10th of the month. 
I which our auto pay was due on the 5th of the month. 
The representative told  me directly that it would be taken care of that she would extend it till the 10th.
Well DON'T TRUST THEM ANY LONGER IT DID NOT HAPPEN IT WAS LIKE A RESPONSE OF TO THE HELL WITH YOU..
DON'T BOTHER ME AND DON'T ASK ME AGAIN. 
nice way of doing business.
Now we have the task of looking into a service we can TRUST.

I contacted T-Mobile for the first and last time. I was interested in switching from Verizon and the first rep I talked to, Joeff told me that up to $800 would be covered of my old plan to switch over to T-Mobile. I said great because we owed over $700 on a phone. Joeff then asked if I needed any upgrades. I told him that I would need to switch out my Pixel 6 for a new Samsung. He then said oh we can give you a free s22. This almost got me to sign right away because he was promising me $700 to switch and an $800 phone. After 2 hours, I was forced to provide my debit card information to set up the order and I was sent the signature email, I noticed that I was not able to view an invoice or read any fine print disclosing any terms or conditions for what I was actually receiving and paying for. When I went to contact customer service to get full disclosure, I was told that no documentation, invoice, or any written documentation of what I am agreeing to and signing would be provided. I was only told that my questions could be answered by the representative. I repeatedly asked for full disclosure and documentation so that I know what I am agreeing to and what comes with this agreement but again zero documentation was provided to me. Instead they repeatedly tried to just send me screenshots with the prices. It took a couple more hours for customer service to let me know that I was misled about the separate offers and either a free phone or $800 to switch would be provided but not both. I was repeatedly told I had to sign the document without reading anything about it and then I would be provided with the fine print and full disclosure that the second representative promised me. After 4 or 5 hours of begging for an invoice, they began to send me links to their phone plans and told me to read and try to find my answers. After wasting all this time and getting my hopes up, I do not trust T-Mobile whatsoever and I feel like people get tricked this way everyday. Joeff talked about Verizon's crappy service but T-Mobile is even worse. Don't waste 5 hours of your time

I upgraded my plan and received a new phone for free.  TheSamsung phone was defective and phone calls just cut out and you couldn't hear the other person.  I had to go pick up another phone a week later and the fingerprint reader was defective.  So the third phone came in and once again I had to go and pick it up.  I noticed the phone was in a box that said refurbished.  The rep said they all come in that way.  Hmmm.  I received a message from Samsung to register my phone.  Lo and behold the warranty was gone because it was too old and refurbished.  I called and I was told that is what T-mobile does,  give you refurbished if your original new phone is defective.  On top of that I paid $43 for a screen protector on the 2nd phone and the store refused to put on a new screen protector on the third phone.  So now I have a used phone with no Samsung warranty, 2 months tmobile warranty and am out $43 for a screen protector that was on my 2nd defective phone for 3 days.  I feel like I have been totally scammed.

I've been a T-Mobile customer since 1999 when you were VoiceStream wireless. I got voicemails that came in yesterday from all the way back in the beginning of July and all you all were  
Able to offer me was a $25 credit. 24 years of doing business with you and that's all you can do. I'm switching to Verizon. If I offered that kind of or the lack there of customer service to one of my customers, I wouldn't have any.

I have been a happy customer of Sprint since 1996. Once T-mobile took over, disasters began. They were shutting down the old Sprint network, and I had 4G phones, so I didn't expect a problem, but they told me I had to switch. They offered me a "free" downgrade from my top of the line LG to a bottom of the line Samsung, but only if i agreed to an obligation to pay for it if I didn't stay a customer for 2 full years.

That wasn't acceptable to me, so I bought some Samsung A53 phones, which is a middle of the line phone. T-mobile couldn't activate them on Sprint. They couldn't activate them even if i changed to t-mobile account. I was even referred to a specialist, who told me it was impossible, because they were the "international version", not the "American version".

I moved to Tello, and the phones were activated without any trouble at all. Ironically, I'm still using the t-mobile network, but at half the price, and service has been flawless.

At about the same time, I mixed up checks, and sent t-mobile a check addressed to the Gas company for $1851.40. They cashed it, even though it was not addressed to them. It is now over two months later, and they are still promising to refund it. I will believe it when I see it.

Throughout this whole process, the customer service reps were awful. Tello customer service, has been so much better. Now it I can just get my refund, I can forget about t-mobile.

Your service sucks!!!!!
Can you hear me now!
I would be surprised if you even get 
This !

today i have the worse service you can get in a phone company Tmobile 3 hours on the phone still no solution i go to Verizon today after finish talking to the representative i mention that and he said go to verizon i i will follow his recommendation

The modem didn't arrive on time for the first installation and the connection of the previous provider stopped. After 4 different contacts and no explanation of what happened in the warehouse for the modem to not be dispatched they sent another hardware which arrived 1 week later than the expected. When the technician arrived to do the installation I was informed that the line is not connected by T-Mobile, called the customer service and again the explanation is that they don't really know what went wrong and that there is no estimation on when it will be fixed. I am now over a week without internet and if this is how a contract starts I would never recommend T-Mobile house to anyone.
How can we as the consumer get rid of this trash service

T-Mobile customer service is awful.  They just tell you things to get you off the phone.  They promise to call you back in an hour and never do.  They double billed me an activation fee and tried to charge my card again for $130.  My bank called with suspicious activity, so I call and they say they don't know anything about a $130 charge, but when I called my bank back they said the charge was cancelled.  More than a coincidence I think!!  I had to cancel my credit card because of it and I had to get a new debit card because mine was set up in autopay and I didn't trust they wouldn't charge me again.  I am still trying to get my money back for the double billing.  I will NEVER use their service again!!!!

Been with T-Mobile for 17 years. Bill has always been paid and then I needed a simple change of name on the account (after my husband died) and you feel like you’re a stranger to them, a scammer or a criminal actually. If you have your credit frozen for security reasons, expect to be put through hell with hours on the phone ( mostly on hold) .. only to find nothing was changed or done, though I was told all was taken care of. FF 10 days and it feels like Groundhog Day .. going throw another afternoon on the phone and on hold. You’re expected to understand all those people with a strong accent and you feel like you died and went to heaven when one of the many people you deal with actually speaks your language .. UNBELIEVABLE RED TAPE for a minor issue !! Why do they require a temporary lift on your credit freeze with ALL 3 credit bureaus.. ?? That’s a task I wish on nobody!! Being a loyal customer means absolutely nothing to them …

If I could leave zero stars...we have had it for roughly 6months. It has been terrible. Super spoty service, and that's with the box having 3bars. Sometimes we'll get good internet of 100mbps down and 36 up. But majority of the time it's been 1up and 1down or have really high latency. Making gaming impossible. We have complained to TMobile. They say to factory reset, oh we are working on the towers in your area, here's a new upgraded box, I don't know why it's not working and will give a discount since you haven't had Internet for 3 days. The discounts were never put in the documentation. We had autopay. They started saying they couldn't take money off my card when it works just fine. Try to get on my account and couldn't because there is a number that comes with the modem they are supposed to tell you to use to get in. So my bill was late bc of the technical issues. I told them. They said they would waive the late fees. Still charged me $72 when it should have been $55. The only good service from them is from the department who's in charge of sending a new box to you. Bottom line:service is spotty, customer support aren't helpful and each claim they are the best in that field, and they lie about fees and discounts.

Update aug. 21, 2022
Our internet went out last night and kept bouncing between no signal and weak. We power cycled it, shut it off for 15mins, and then factory reset it. Still not working. Called this morning. They don't have my name or email on the account anymore as an authorized person. They said  towers were all down for updates (been told this the past 4 times we called but I guess if it's gonna make it better then I'm fine). They said they are going to refund me 25$ for the inconveniences. We shall see. I'll update more when I find out...

I traded my phone in on a promotion that I could save by changing internet service. I found out the internet was not the price they quoted I called to cancel but was told to wait they would escalate this and I would be given the price I was sold. This went on for 2 months. I have been offered the same internet service at the same price two more times since by tmobile but after over 2 months of going back and forth was told they will not ho or the price. I think the whole deal should be undone. I wouldn't have sold my phone had I known and after months I can't get the internet plan I canceled for tmobile offer they will not honor. The entire deal should be undone they broke the contract but yet I am stuck finding a new internet service now and paying monthly for a phone that was supposed to be paid with the saving of me switching. Be wary what they say they are selling you may not be true.  I will pay for over a year extra money now because I trusted them for the deal then for 2 months to make it right. They fell short on both. They say the offer wad a mistake but I have been offered the internet service for that same price two times since so they are continuing to fraud people on the deal.

Absolute worst service !! Bill has increased 50 a month ( 3 lines) and never have data (I pay for unlimited) rarely have signal (for calling)!! I will be disconnecting my services with them ASAP!! Spotty services in the major city 15 min from home. only time we (3 lines) can use our Unlimited plan phones is on wifi !! typing this out on a desk top computer because my google 6 phone I paid 600(?) for is a useless paper weight. this has been an ongoing issue for nearly a year and tmobile refuses to be held accountable for my lack of services, and my bill has progressively increaed over the last 18 months to the point it is nearly more than my car payment!! what horrible company to claim leader in anything.

such bad internet, it says 5g but feels like 4g-2g. When more than 3 devices are connected to it, every device gets around 2-15fps and 500 ping at least. DO NOT GET THIS INTERNET "SERVICE". T-Mobile is complete garbage, they kill my phones battery, my battery drains 5% in five minutes when I’m connected Wi-Fi very annoying, they also have terrible service also annoying, I have an iPhone X, gonna have to switch soon because T-Mobile is complete trash

The customer service is a joke and they give you the run around. They tell you they are there to help you but they don’t explain the process. I’ve called 4 times for the same issue and it is still not resolved. I will not use T-Mobile for services and will not recommend.

If I could you would get NO STARS... This is the worst service... When we first got the phones/service it was great but the last 2 months have been miserable...no matter where I am at.. WASTE OF MONEY!!!!!

T-Mobile offered us a free phone as compensation for a billing error in which they extracted hundreds of dollars from us over a period of months. Now they are claiming that to unlock this (supposedly free) phone so that it works when not connected to wifi (which we were assured it would by multiple customer service representatives), we need to either pay $1,000 or wait 24 months. They are refusing to unlock the phone until we pay the full cost of the phone. In short, T-Mobile employees convinced us to trade in a working phone for a new one-- only to discover the new one will not work for two years. To fix the issue we are now obligated to pay $1,000. If you can avoid working with Sprint/T-Mobile I advise you to stay far away!

I would not even give them one star but you have to at least place one star to comment. This company is a  Disgusting, underhanded company with many shady sides. This company cannot be trusted. My phone popped up with “network error”, and their only response is to buy a new phone. Why would I do that if the network is the error? July 13th my account was compromised. Spent many hours on hold as well as communicating via FB messenger. After wasting hours of my time trying to fix the issue, I was told they had it corrected and would "escalate" my account to the fraud unit. For some reason a TMobile representative cancelled my account instead of reporting it as fraud and contacting me to change my personal Information. They would credit 1 month on my account for my trouble. Yesterday, I received a text that they were charging me the amount they were supposed to credit, plus an additional $10. 
I called today regarding the charges and am not able to access my TMobile account, nor can they access it. I had been on Hold over 3 hours and have been transferred several times, escalated to a supervisor call, and hung up on. I am typing this message while on hold. I called TMobile again and am again transferred to a Supervisor. He informed me my account was never reported to the fraud department. Then in mid sentence put me on hold. I was on hold for over 2 hours and he just hung up on me. Now I will have to report to my credit agencies and my bank due to their errors. Talk about security and data breach! I will be going with another company as soon as I get this straightened out.

So there was a promotion in may for trade any qualifying phone in and receive 1000.00 towards a new samsung s22 of your choice. ok so I had a note 9 that qualified for the promotion. so i traded my note 9 in on an s22 ultra. i should have only had to pay 200.00 for it after the 1000.00.
the cost of the phone was 1199.00. my husband had no trade in. so he got the s21 that was supposed to be free with a new line. ok so we did both. and let me tell you how much info and stuff i lost because Trevor, the manager, wouldn't let the transfer finish because he was in a bigg ass hurry. so my bill shows a buy 1 get 1 free. wait a minute, I never did a b1g1. if i was gonna do a b1g1 i sure wouldn't pick the cheapest and already free phone for my free phone. i would have gotten 2 s22's. So now, not only am I paying full price on the s22 ultra, I'm somehow getting the same free s21 free again, and they say I traded in my note 9 on the free s21 phone. so I should have 2 free phones, but I don't. now if that ain't a rip off and scam I don't know what is. but I signed the right contract. and how convenient that nobody can find these signed documents that I signed at the Vernal, ut office with Trevor and Kelly. and they said oh if you have problems come talk to us. it's the end of August and I have been complaining about this since I got my first bill and noticed the promotion was completely wrong. I called them, they never answer their phone, and went in to talk to them in Vernal numerous times. the issue never gets fixed. so I'm thinking that the Vernal office is the scammers. oh and then let me tell y'all how rude they were to my son when I took him in to sign up for service. they made fun of his names, and said what kind of parent gives their kid 4 names. well this parent does. very uncalled for to do to a future customer. I should have walked out instead of letting my son sign up for their service.

Awful experience with this company. If you’re looking to get a service plan stay away from these sharks. I joined s month ago and only received an email say welcome blah, blah, blah. Well I wasn’t told we needed unlocked phones, but they could get us free ones but had to pay the taxes. Well the next day the phone order was canceled and not a call from the supervisor or team I worked with apologizing and trying to make things right. They’re superficial customer service needs some well needed adjustments. If you’re looking to spend s lot of money and not get anything back, call me. I can easily take money and not do anything for it. Ha ha. 
Hang tight my American brothers and sisters because companies like this will be their own demise. Let’s bring back honesty together.

Over charged,
I will be leaving this provider as soon as I m finishing paying for this over priced substandard phone  and able to take my old number with me... they got me trapped for the moment but not for long!

If I could give them a zero I would. I purchased four lines from T mobile. I should have known they would be trash when I couldn’t activate my phones on the website to save the $35 activation fee per line. I was supposed to receive trade in credit for each line. They only sent me shipping labels for 3 out of the 4 lines. Then, I had problems porting in my numbers. I contacted them at least three times trying to get that issue resolved. They told me they would credit me back for the time I couldn’t use the phones. That was a joke. When I realized they didn’t send me the 4th shipping label, I contacted them. They never sent me the label. I had to go out of town for surgery, so I asked for an extension sending the phones back. They told me okay and they would rectifying the shipping label situation and apply the promotion to all phones. They were supposed to call me back to send the labels and they didn’t. When I contacted them back, I was told I couldn’t get full trade in value because I was outside the return window even though I asked for an extension. I was never given that information from the previous representative. Then I was told they wouldn’t take my 4th phone for the trade in promotion. To get out of the contract, I would have to pay full price for the phone and for the service, over $3,300. Like dating, I should have taken heed to the warning signs before committing. DO NOT USE T MOBILE!!

SENIORS BEWARE!  We signed up for the 55+ Plan. Set up for automatic pay and forgot about it. One day I noticed my payments had jumped over %50! When I looked into it...they raised the rate after the first year...get this...because every year you have to send in proof that you are STILL over 55! We got no notice. So we paid the new rate for many months! Then, when I provided proof, the rate went down, slightly, but not down to the original rate plan. If there is a way to get YOUNGER every year and no longer qualify for the plan...someone, please let me know!!!!

Craziest  company there ever was when it comes to customer appreciation. Also lied to us about a delivery we were expecting turned out they never did. But 3 people told me it was on the way. I have had numerous frustrations with T-mobile, mainly due to poor cell phone service, calls or texts not coming through, or being very delayed like for hours, and dishonest customer service both in the store and via phone (I was told one thing and received another). 

Having been fed up, I cancelled my serviced before June 28th. When I called to cancel and ask for the instructions to ensure I had cancelled, I was given a “key” to transfer my phone number to the new carrier. I was told that I would NOT need to call back that when I transferred the number my T-mobile service would automatically be cancelled. 

However, I was billed $60.20 on July 10th for a service I did not use. (I would have understood a pro-rating of my service as I had stopped using it on June 28th, but this was my previous monthly amount). I called to explain and asked that they correct the billing. I was told due to their “policy” they could not refund the money. I said if he would not, then I would take it up with my bank. He said that I should take it up with my bank. So I did, and on July 14th I received a refund thanks to my bank. 

A couple days later I called back to request confirmation that my service was indeed cancelled and that I would no longer be charged. I asked for an email to confirm in writing that my service was indeed cancelled. She put me on hold and after 45 minutes the call was dropped and there was no follow up via phone or email. Now today, July 22nd, I received a bill from T-mobile for $90.20. I do not owe them any money as I had auto-pay and was never late, and the phone I had purchased was returned unopened. 

I filed a dispute with the BBB because I have been unfairly billed $90.20 and I want my name to be clear. 

I will NEVER use t-mobile services again and will tell everyone who mentions needing a cell service to avoid t-mobile.

On July 5,2022I went into the TMobile store. I asked the asst. Manager Ethan that if I couldn't get service can I bring them back and get my money back , he yes . Phones don't work, tried everything and finally after days said that the location of my house didn't get a signal. So take my phones back. Sturgis store said absolutely not. I called into main line, they say yes I get refunded all my money back. STILL WAITING. now your saying another two weeks 🤬. I'll call everyday until I do. Then if I don't lawyer.

Because T-mobile couldn't provide reliable service where we moved to, we had to switch to a different carrier. On the same day (5/17/2022) when the new service was ordered, we cancelled (not suspended) our service. We actually did it from the new service office while there. Today, 7/22/2022, after receiving one bill after another, and talking to over 7 different people from India to Georgia USA, I was finally able to get someone to "cancel" my account only to find out they still want a "last payment" from me.  Their customer service (when you can understand them) is horrible, their motives are unclear at the best, and their business practice is the worst. I used to think I would always have them as my carrier, I now know that I will never have them again.

i have many years with this carrier, and every time I try to get new cellphones with "promotions" firs two months same pay, after that payments got up, and up. I'm tired of them. they make me feel stupid

Came into T-mobile store to just get an updated version of my Samsung phone. But when updating customized settings with the new phone, it kept sending code to my OLD phone (which no longer "exists")!
WHAT?  I COULDN'T EVEN CHANGE THE WALLPAPER.

When I explained this to the T-mobile guys, they didn't know how to fix (said it is a problem with Samsung) and just suggested not changing anything on the phone. Crazy!  In fact one guy even suggested that I would be PAYING $25/MONTH FOR THE SAMSUNG ACCOUNT if they got it working!! NOT TRUE.

The core issue is they didn't understand that the "Samsung Account" is linked with my "Google email/password".  One of the guys got to a point at which you can change the device for where CODE is sent (was able to send to my cell phone number instead of a previous phone). Wow! It worked and everything unlocked so I can have a fully functional phone now. 

Took over an hour of frustration though because we kept being told they couldn't fix it.  WHAT? BRAND NEW PHONE WITH LIMITED FUNCTIONALITY???? I worry about how many people would have believed this?

PLEASE TRAIN STAFF BETTER. If they don't have a quick fix, they should go on the web. Get educated.  Create a troubleshooting blog, etc.

I recently dropped AT&T for 2 main reasons. 
#1 Because they continue to increase my bill without explanation when it should have been dropping. 
#2 because their Customer Service is the worst!
About 6 months ago opened an account with T-Mobile for home Wi-Fi which I am very happy with so since I was leaving AT&T, I decided to go to T-Mobile for my phones. 
 I needed 3 phones. One for my wife, daughter and for myself. We spent 3 hours in the store which is not my complaint. My complaint is that they sold us a phone with a cracked screen. This crack is so fine that we were unable to see it before leaving the store. This crack is one that was not caused from an impact but more of a break from assembly. 
 After leaving the store we stopped to grab a bite to eat near the T-Mobile store when my daughter notice we first thought it was just a scratch. We called the store and they said that they didn't have anymore of the Green IPhones in stock and said to bring the phone back in on Monday which my daughter did. They said that they still did not have any green phones so my daughter asked if she could get a blue phone instead. They told her that they can only replace it like for like.  They told her that they would call her when they get new phones which has been 12 days now.
 I called customer service and they said that there was nothing they can do, they said that if I would like, they will sale me damage insurance for $90 and we can take this busted phone to the Apple Store and they would replace the glass. I don't know about anyone else but to me, I did not spend a $1000 for a repaired phone! This is Big Tech taking advantage of the public and I hope this review spreads like wild fire. 
 What is very disturbing is that when we bought the phones, they first said that per their computer they only had one green phone in stock but the salesperson said let me check the safe. Low and behold they had another. What is even more frustrating is that the first CSR I worked with, she said that it was odd that they didn't have any of the Green IPhones left when she was seeing that they had multiple phones.

This company is without a doubt the worst company I have ever dealt with.  They can’t get my bill correct after 5 straight months of phone calls.  Somehow them misplacing my trade in cellphones became a “Me” problem.  Their incompetence is never ending.

Wish there was an option to give 0 stars to their customer service. Everytime I call their customer service number I end up getting a representative who can't even speak fluent English. Leave aside, me understanding what they are speaking and they wouldn't understand the basic English words I would end up using. Please get your phone customer service US based OR atleast outsource to a country where people can speak fluent English OR make sure the Tmobile/outsourcing vendor has qualified English speaking reps.

If I could give T Mobile a negative rating, I would.  Never have I ever had a more negative customer service experience in my 58 years. I contacted them because my bill keeps jumping 43 dollars.  I’m on the 50+ year old plan with two lines.  Ive verified my age 4 times now, twice at the brick and mortar store and twice via phone/ email.  They, however, continue telling me I must verify my age.  I asked whom they know that ages younger, to only receive a perplexed response.  

Also, I migrated over to T Mobile from Sprint about 6 months ago….guess what!   No I didn’t according to them.  Ive been informed that I now have multiple calls to make….nope, not gonna happen.

I’ve spoken to 7 reps today and none have been able to resolve this issue,

You would think in the middle of ALL this so-called modern technology - this GERMAN multinational FREEmasonry corporation could design a black CUBE (sacred geometry used for rituals) which is more energy efficient (router has added $100.00 to electric statement) and without exposing hue-mans to exceedingly high amounts of radiation but don't expect this corporation to have any morals or ethics!

There is an issue with the tmobile network. 4g service stopped working at my house. So did a metropcs phone at the same time. Coincidence? No. Customer service is horrendous.  Dropped calls and countless calls and no one seems to know what they are doing. 3 weeks later still no service unless i drive a few miles away.

I've been with T Mobile for 13+ years. Customer service used to be impeccable, but for the last couple of years it's been in the toilet. I soent over 6 HOURS today trying to rectify an issue. I was utilizing both phone and chat. On Twitter, I was switched between 3 different people, with no notice (I'm assuming they went on break or off shift), in the TM app, the reps lied to me. It is now 12:45 AM CDT, I started the process at 6:00 PM CDT. I am so disgusted by TM and I'm ready to switch providers.

Never bought a phone and being billed a monthly plan of 105.00 for phones I never bought and service I don't have! Worst experience ever! Customer service is terrible and they wont do anything without an imaginary pin# created at the time I didn't buy the phones. BBB and lawyer next!

Team Mobile Bad experience switching to another provider. They lied to me no cost for upgrading my phone went to the store $1,100.00  No Thanks switching to AT&T more money but less headaches and will be no charge for the phone. You should do the same.

Came into the Store. Waited 30 mins for someobe to help me. Two people where being helped and one employee was just standing around. Not on the phone or computer they where just there standing up doing nothing. I litetally just came in to make a payment and had to wait 30 mins?!!?? I feel that T-mobile can do ALOT better. It is u nrofessional in so many levels.

I have been with T-mobile for 17 yrs and the last 2 years has been terrible customer service I hate calling I can't understand nothing they are saying and they screw up really bad, PLEASE BRING BACK THE OLD T-MOBILE CUSTOMER SERVICE!!!!!!
